soap:
RE-REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7463 — feat(index): read a column's min/max from ZoneMap without a scan
reviewed `1fc46c07` → `96cab339`  (2 commits, 4 files; 2 base-merge files excluded from delta)

**S — Subjective**

I last looked at this at `1fc46c07` and **approved it** ("This seems good."). My only feedback was a single **nitpick, non-blocking**: I didn't love hanging another accessor method off the top-level `Dataset` object, and suggested "some sort of `Dataset::statistics()` handle this was applied to." The author (Ali2Arslan) engaged in-thread, proposed two options (a `DatasetStatisticsExt` extension trait vs. a wrapper struct around `Dataset`), and I did not reply to pick one; they chose the wrapper-struct route on their own and pushed it, then asked me to confirm it addresses the comment. The delta since my approval is exactly that refactor plus a doc-comment pass. No new feature work, no logic changes to the soundness core.

**O — Objective**

Prior conditions ledger: **empty**. My last review carried zero `issue(blocking)` conditions — it was an approve with one nitpick — so the packet's 0-condition ledger is accurate, not a reconstruction miss. I verified this against the actual posted review/comment set (one `APPROVED` review, one inline `nitpick:` comment thread, no blocking tokens anywhere).

Delta contents, verified against the patch and the fetched head sources:
- `rust/lance/src/dataset.rs` (+7): adds `Dataset::statistics(&self) -> DatasetStatistics<'_>`.
- `rust/lance/src/dataset/statistics.rs` (+98/-1): new `DatasetStatistics<'a>` handle (`#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]`, borrows `&'a Dataset`) carrying `column_value_range`. This is the code that previously lived as `DatasetIndexExt::zonemap_value_range`/`column_value_range` on the dataset, moved verbatim in logic.
- `rust/lance/src/index/scalar.rs` (+17/-5): tests only — rewired `ds.column_value_range(..)` call sites to `ds.statistics().column_value_range(..)`. Same three assertions (basic range, uncovered-append → `None`, multi-segment fold), same expected values.
- `rust/lance-index/src/scalar/zonemap.rs` (+1/-2): one doc-comment reword on `scalar_is_finite_bound`. No logic change.

Merge state (live at build): CI **green**, mergeable **true**.

The soundness-bearing code (`value_range_over` fold with NaN-max bail and finite-bound gating; the trait `value_range` default returning `None`; the single-segment `value_range` delegating to `value_range_over([self])`) is **unchanged in this delta** — I confirmed the fetched head bodies match what I approved at `1fc46c07`. The joint-fragment-coverage gate in `column_value_range` (union of segment `fragment_bitmap`s, `None` on any missing bitmap, `dataset.fragment_bitmap.is_subset(&covered)`) is also carried over unchanged and still guarded by the appended-fragment test.

**A — Assessment**

The one piece of feedback I left has been addressed the way I asked for. `column_value_range` now lives behind a dedicated `DatasetStatistics` handle obtained from `Dataset::statistics()` instead of accreting a method on `Dataset` — that is precisely the "statistics handle" I gestured at. The handle is a zero-cost `Copy` borrow wrapper with a `pub(crate)` constructor, which is the right shape: it groups future index-derived, no-scan accessors without widening the `Dataset` surface.

Fresh-eyes pass over everything that changed since my approval turns up **no new concerns**. The refactor is a pure move: the tests are the same assertions rerouted through the handle, and the delta touches no fold/soundness logic. The doc rewording on `scalar_is_finite_bound` and the `column_value_range` rustdoc are accurate to the code (NaN bail, joint coverage, superset-under-deletes framing all match the implementation).

Two non-blocking observations, both pre-existing (not introduced by this delta), noted only so they carry forward:
- `column_value_range` returns `Err(invalid_input)` for an unknown column but `Ok(None)` for a known column with no ZoneMap. The asymmetry is defensible and is pinned by the retained `"other" → Ok(None)` test; not something to act on.
- Codecov flags patch coverage at ~94% with the uncovered lines being the early-return guards (missing-`fragment_bitmap` → `None`, downcast-failure → `None`). These are hard-to-hit defensive branches; acceptable.

Nothing here needs a sitting. This was already an approve; the delta only makes it cleaner.

**P — Plan**

Confirm to the author that the wrapper-struct approach addresses the nitpick, and re-affirm the approval. No blocking items. No conditions to carry.

RECOMMENDATION: approve
confidence: high · what would make this wrong: if the logic move into `DatasetStatistics` silently altered the fold or coverage-gate behavior — but I verified the head bodies are identical to the approved version and the same tests pass green.
summary: Delta since my approval (1fc46c07->96cab339) is exactly the refactor I asked for as a nitpick: column_value_range moved off Dataset onto a new DatasetStatistics handle via Dataset::statistics(), plus a doc-comment pass. Prior ledger had zero blocking conditions (approve + one nitpick), so nothing to verify met/not-met. Fresh-eyes pass over the moved code: pure move, soundness-bearing fold/coverage-gate logic unchanged, tests rerouted with identical assertions, CI green. Re-affirming approve.

comments.label: praise
comments.body: praise: This is exactly the `Dataset::statistics()` handle I was hoping for — grouping no-scan, index-derived accessors behind a `Copy` borrow wrapper instead of adding another method to `Dataset`. Addresses my earlier nitpick. Thanks for picking a direction and running with it.
